task: Squad Landing v7 - Full editorial luxury redesign (Next.js 14 + Tailwind + Framer Motion)
what_worked: Full stack pivot: dropped Three.js/GSAP/Lenis → Framer Motion only (simpler, smaller)
Editorial luxury direction: Playfair Display + near-black palette + minimal animation
2×2 uniform agent grid (Xiaosong's hard requirement) - enforced by grid uniformity rule from spec
8 sections built in one pass: Nav → Hero → Agents → PullQuote → Pipeline → Terminal → CTA → Footer
Gatekeeper first-round REVISE then APPROVED after 4 required + 3 nice-to-have fixes
Terminal staggered line fade (delay: i * 0.04) added as nice-to-have, improved UX significantly
what_failed: Initial build had placeholder # hrefs in Nav, CTA, Footer - Gatekeeper caught all 3
Terminal dots used colored macOS style (red/yellow/green) instead of muted gray - spec required editorial restraint
Bundle landed at 126KB vs 120KB target (6KB Framer Motion overhead - accepted as acceptable)
PullQuote missing apostrophe: slippin. → slippin' - caught by Gatekeeper
lesson: Real URLs from day 1. Don't ship placeholder # hrefs to Gatekeeper - it's always caught. Design consistency matters: colored terminal dots broke the editorial luxury aesthetic even if functional. Check spec for every decorative element.
gatekeeper_verdict: REVISE then APPROVED (1 revision round, 7 file changes)
